identity.name: Mike Garza
identity.title: Owner / Shop Manager

pbContactData.summary: 18 years in precision CNC machining. Started at Boeing as a machinist, opened Garza Precision in 2014 with savings and an SBA loan. Runs a 5-machine shop specializing in aerospace brackets and hydraulic manifolds. Active NTMA chapter member.

identity.fitReason: 5-machine CNC shop, aerospace + hydraulic work, high air demand, current compressor undersized

rapport_starters: NTMA member — ask about the last chapter meeting
Aerospace brackets = tight tolerances, mention clean dry air
18 yrs machining — respect the craft, skip the sales pitch
Started his own shop in 2014 — self-made, hates corporate fluff
Hydraulic manifolds = oily environment, filtration matters
intel_nuggets: Running 5 CNC machines (Haas VF-2 x3, Mazak QTN-200 x2) — needs 50-75 CFM at 125 PSI. Current 25HP recip is short-cycling.
Aerospace work — AS9100 environment. Clean dry air is a compliance requirement, not a nice-to-have.
Shop is in Mesa, AZ (east Phoenix metro) — check local service availability.
Owner-operator with 15 employees, $2.4M revenue. He makes the decisions.
Current piston compressor is ~7 years old, having moisture and short-cycling issues. Ripe for upgrade.
rapport.opening_line: Hey Mike, I noticed you run some aerospace bracket work — I had a question about how you handle air quality for those tight tolerance jobs.
rapport.adapted_script: Start with air quality angle (aerospace = clean dry air requirement). DO NOT lead with price or energy savings — shop owners running aerospace work care about quality and uptime first. If he mentions his current compressor, ask about short-cycling and moisture issues. The JRS-10E rotary screw is the natural recommendation for 5 machines with variable demand. Key value props: energy savings vs piston, integrated dryer option, AS9100-grade air quality, 5-year airend warranty. Avoid: sounding scripted or corporate.
watch_outs: He built this shop himself — respect independence, don't be pushy
Aerospace QMS compliance is sensitive — ask, don't assume which standard
Metroplex Air Systems has called him before — he knows local competitors exist
Don't oversell or make claims you can't back up
product_reference: JRS-10E — 10HP, 42 CFM @ 125 PSI, 460V/3ph, $9,495. Enclosed rotary screw.
JDD-40 — Desiccant dryer, -60°F dewpoint, wall mount, $7,495. Molecular sieve media (not activated alumina) — deeper moisture removal, billet aluminum housing, 1/3 size of conventional. For AS9100.
JRD-40 — Refrigerated dryer, 38°F dewpoint, 40 CFM, 115V, $2,495. General shop use.
JCF-70 — Coalescing filter, 0.01 micron, $349. Oil-free air for aerospace.
JPF-70 — Particulate pre-filter, 1 micron, $399. Upstream of dryer.

interactions.summary: Opened VSD rotary screw compressor email 3x — strong interest
Clicked link to compressor sizing calculator

icpScore.fit_reason: 5-machine CNC shop, aerospace + hydraulic verticals, high air demand, Mesa AZ metro, owner-operator decision maker
